behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "includes data retrieved from all three sources." The failure boundary is module handoff/composition (3 source clients -> aggregation payload -> summary step), not a single pure function, so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA: no phrase requires real OS/network/subprocess; stubbed source adapters can reproduce omission/merge bugs, so System is rejected. LLM-DEP: this checks pre-LLM input payload completeness, not model judgment, so Agentic is rejected. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with test doubles for the three source clients returning distinct sentinel fields; invoke report build and assert the exact payload sent to summary-generation contains all three source sections/keys.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note ... rather than aborting." This is cross-module error propagation/fallback behavior (source adapter failure -> report assembly), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require proving real network/OS failure modes; an in-process adapter throwing timeout/unavailable errors can expose abort-vs-degrade bugs, so System is rejected. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback note insertion, not model output quality, so Agentic is rejected. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic sequence, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source clients returning normal data and one client raising an unavailable/timeout error; assert generation completes, includes the `data unavailable` marker in that section, and does not terminate the run.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "narrative summary accurately represents the key values." This is output-quality/faithfulness of generated language, which depends on real model decisions (LLM-DEP). A stubbed model would always pass and hide hallucination/omission failures (MOCK-HIDE), so Integration is too low. REAL-INFRA/System is not the key boundary here; correctness hinges on model output content. MULTI-STEP: behavior mentions one summary step, not a chain of agentic stages, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on representative input fixtures with explicit key metrics/issues/deploy facts; score outputs for factual inclusion/consistency against those keys (e.g., required fact checks), failing if key values are omitted, altered, or contradicted.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly." The failure boundary is real external delivery/inbox behavior (protocol/auth/routing/spam handling), which mocks can hide (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE), so Integration is too low. Agentic was considered because summary generation exists in pipeline, but rejected by behavior text focus on delivery outcome, not model output quality; this is infrastructure E2E, not LLM judgment. MULTI-STEP agentic chain is not required, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end against test instances of the three data sources and a real test mailbox/service path; verify a message is actually received by the configured inbox with expected report structure/content sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "email delivery service is unreachable" and "retried on the next scheduled run." This requires real infrastructure boundaries: network-level delivery failure plus cross-run persistence/rehydration behavior; mocks can miss real connection error classes and run-to-run state issues (REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). Integration was considered, but rejected because behavior explicitly includes unreachable delivery service and next scheduled execution semantics. LLM-DEP is not central (no model-quality assertion), and no multi-agentic chain, so Agentic/Workflow are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Execute run 1 with email endpoint intentionally unreachable (real refused connection/timeout) and confirm report is persisted to local fallback file/spool. Execute run 2 (separate invocation simulating next schedule) with email service restored; assert persisted report is retried and delivered, and fallback state is cleared/updated correctly.
